steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage says sellers benefit by prompting consumers to consider their own valuations. The blank must complete this idea using graph data. The "pay what you think it's worth" message directly prompts valuation-thinking. In the graph, that condition produces the highest mean prices in both studies: roughly 54 dollars (online group) and 74 dollars (students), compared to 27 to 40 dollars for the other two messages. The correct answer must show (1) both groups priced higher under "pay what you think it's worth" and (2) heterogeneity between the two groups.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — the students tended to value the concert tickets more highly than did the more age-diverse group recruited online, but when considering what they could afford to pay, the students tended to choose a lower price than did the other group.: The graph shows students chose a higher price (about 40 dollars) than the online group (about 30 dollars) in the "pay what you can" condition — this statement reverses that relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — in all three messaging conditions, the group of participants recruited online tended to choose lower prices than did the students, but both groups tended to choose prices closest to the actual cost of the tickets when prompted to consider the tickets' value.: Neither the text nor the graph mentions the actual market price of the tickets, so there is no basis for the second claim. ✗
  • Choice C — the students tended to value the concert tickets more highly than did the more age-diverse group recruited online, but both groups tended to choose a higher price when considering the value of the tickets than when considering what they could afford or wanted to pay.: Students did price higher overall (heterogeneity), and both groups' highest prices came under the "pay what you think it's worth" condition — exactly what the graph shows. This confirms the passage's point about valuation-prompting benefiting sellers. ✓
  • Choice D — within each group of participants, there was wide variation in the value that individuals assigned to the concert tickets, but the students tended to assign a higher value to the tickets than did the more age-diverse group recruited online.: The graph shows mean prices per condition, not individual variance within groups, so the claim about "wide variation within each group" is unsupported. ✗
C is correct.
